Explanation: This data audit report demonstrates strong technical execution and clarity. The three-step methodology is well-structured and systematic, effectively identifying critical data integrity failures (100% missingness in process metadata, 65.6% in concentration variables, and physically impossible negative values). The writing is generally clear with logical organization from abstract through conclusion. Visual elements (Figure 1) effectively support key findings—the histogram clearly shows negative value artifacts, and the scatter plot convincingly demonstrates unit mislabeling through strong linear correlation. The scientific rigor is solid, with appropriate statistical analysis (missingness percentages, completeness metrics) and sound exclusion criteria. Code listings add reproducibility. Minor limitations include: the report could provide more granular statistical detail (distribution statistics, correlation coefficients), limited discussion of root causes or mitigation strategies, and the constraint to non-imputation may be presented as more absolute than necessary. The originality is moderate—while the structured audit approach is competent, it represents standard data quality assessment practice rather than innovative methodology.
